---
title: "Figure 4—figure supplement 4 — sample and correlation distortion vs channel count, resolved by noise: R and IR split on the correlation part, and noise cures it only at few channels"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
THE MECHANISM AS A SHAPE, resolved by instrumental noise. Supplement 3 shows on k_off that the total
distortion splits as total ~ sample x correlation and that R's floor is the correlation part. This
supplement generalises that to the two carrier directions (k_off and N_ch) AND adds the noise axis,
because the noise dependence answers a question Figure 4 leaves open: does raising the instrumental
noise cure R?

  x = N_ch (log)          y = distortion (null = 1)
  rows    = parameter (k_off, N_ch), each split into its two components (sample, correlation)
  columns = instrumental noise (0.1, 1, 10)
  colour  = algorithm (R, IR), both in every panel
  band    = interquartile range over the 7 intervals (delta is marginalised into the band; the
            delta dependence is non-monotone and carries the kappa ~ 1e5 corner, so it is left to
            supplement 2's maps rather than shown as a cut)

TWO readings, one figure:

  1. THE SPLIT (compare the sample rows with the correlation rows). In the sample rows R and IR lie on
     top of each other and both go to 1 with N_ch: the per-sample Gaussian approximation becomes
     exact for both, so they do NOT differ in per-sample fidelity. In the correlation rows R stays
     elevated and RISES with N_ch while IR descends to 1. The difference between R and IR is entirely
     correlation.

  2. DOES NOISE CURE R (read the three columns left to right, correlation rows). R's correlation
     distortion starts LOWER at higher noise (at few channels the instrumental noise dominates the
     gating variance, so there is little to distort), but it RE-EMERGES as N_ch grows and reaches
     ~1.3-1.5 at N_ch = 10000 at every noise level. So noise cures R only at FEW channels; because
     gating variance grows with N_ch, the correlation distortion returns at high channel count no
     matter the noise. This is the quantitative form of the band-B/band-C boundary the plan asks
     paper 1 to state: raising noise past the gating variance removes the need, but the gating
     variance is itself a function of N_ch.

More channels therefore do not rescue R: they push the sample part toward 1 and the correlation part
up by the same amount, leaving the total flat (Figure 4's 1.44 floor). The correlation is a property
of the temporal sampling that the interval conditioning removes, in both carrier directions.

DATA: battery_pool_G (theta_pool). Gaussian family: Gaussian_Sample_Distortion and
Likelihood_Correlation_Distortion. Four decades of N_ch x three noise levels. No new runs. Do NOT use
Likelihood_Information_Distortion_Reconstituted (false product identity, NaN here).

TRAP: the "noise" label is not the physical noise. current_noise = label/1000; the y-column here is
the label. TRAP: R's N_ch = 10000 sample band widens because the Delta*k_off = 1 interval sits at the
kappa ~ 1e5 corner (sample deflates to ~0.44 there); that is the interval spread showing honestly as
a band.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
suppressMessages({ library(tidyverse) })
source("figure_4_supp_common.R")
```

```{r data}
COMPS    <- c(sample = SAMP, corr = CORR)
COMP_LAB <- c(sample = "sample", corr = "correlation")
PARAMS   <- c(off = 1, Num_ch_mean = 5)             # the two carrier directions
PAR_LAB  <- c(off = "k[off]", Num_ch_mean = "N[ch]")
ALGO_COL <- c(R = "#D55E00", IR = "#0072B2")

# all three noise levels; collapse the 7 intervals to a median and an IQR band
d <- pmap_dfr(CELLS, function(algo, nch, z)
    read.csv(findf(nch, algo, z, "battery_pool_G"), skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
      mutate(algo = algo, noise = z)) %>%
  filter(component_path %in% COMPS, param_index == param_col, param_index %in% PARAMS,
         statistic == "value", probit == "mean") %>%
  mutate(comp = names(COMPS)[match(component_path, COMPS)]) %>%
  group_by(comp, algo, noise, Num_ch, param_index) %>%
  summarise(med = median(value), lo = quantile(value, .25), hi = quantile(value, .75),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(alab  = factor(ALGO_LAB[algo], levels = c("R", "IR")),
         param = names(PARAMS)[match(param_index, PARAMS)],
         # row = parameter (outer) then component (inner): sample and correlation adjacent per param
         prow  = factor(sprintf("atop(%s, \"%s\")", PAR_LAB[param], COMP_LAB[comp]),
                        levels = as.vector(t(outer(names(PARAMS), names(COMPS),
                          function(p, c) sprintf("atop(%s, \"%s\")", PAR_LAB[p], COMP_LAB[c]))))),
         ncol  = factor(sprintf("sigma == %s", noise),
                        levels = sprintf("sigma == %s", NOISES)),
         lx = log10(Num_ch))
stopifnot(nrow(d) > 0, !any(is.na(d$prow)), !any(is.na(d$ncol)))
cat("supp4:", nrow(d), "curve points; k_off & N_ch, N_ch", paste(NCHS, collapse = "/"),
    "x noise", paste(NOISES, collapse = "/"), "\n")
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 6.6}
X_BRK <- log10(NCHS)
g <- ggplot(d, aes(lx, med, colour = alab, fill = alab)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour = "grey55", linewidth = .3) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = .18, colour = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = .5) + geom_point(size = .8) +
  facet_grid(prow ~ ncol, labeller = label_parsed) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = X_BRK,
                     labels = c("10", expression(10^2), expression(10^3), expression(10^4))) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = ALGO_COL) + scale_fill_manual(values = ALGO_COL) +
  labs(x = expression(N[ch]), y = "distortion (null = 1)", colour = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 8, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(0.14, "cm"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 7.5), strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 6), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 5.5),
        legend.position = "bottom", legend.key.size = unit(.6, "lines"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 7.5), legend.box.spacing = unit(2, "pt"),
        plot.margin = margin(3, 4, 2, 3))
g
ggsave("Figure_4_supplement_4.pdf", g, width = 7.0, height = 6.6)
cat("Figure_4_supplement_4.pdf: 4 x 3 line panels (param x component rows, noise columns), height 6.6 in\n")
```

```{r caption-numbers}
cat("\n=== supp 4 caption numbers ===\n")
for (pp in names(PARAMS)) for (cc in c("sample", "corr")) {
  cat(sprintf("\n--- %s, %s : median vs N_ch, by noise ---\n", PAR_LAB[pp], COMP_LAB[cc]))
  print(as.data.frame(d %>% filter(param == pp, comp == cc) %>%
    mutate(algo = as.character(alab)) %>%
    select(algo, noise, Num_ch, med) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = med) %>%
    arrange(algo, as.numeric(noise))), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)
}
cat("\nREAD: sample rows -> R and IR agree and go to 1. correlation rows -> R rises with N_ch, IR to 1.\n")
cat("Across noise columns: R's correlation starts lower at higher noise but re-emerges at high N_ch.\n")
```
